title: Property-Based Testing
content:
Property-based testing generates random inputs and verifies that certain properties always hold. Instead of writing specific test cases, you describe invariants that should be true for all inputs. This finds edge cases you would never think to test manually.

The proptest crate is the most popular choice. It generates random values, runs your test many times, and if it finds a failure, it automatically shrinks the input to the smallest failing case.
keyPoints: Property tests verify invariants across randomly generated inputs
proptest automatically shrinks failing inputs to minimal cases
Use regex-like patterns to generate strings: "[a-z]{1,20}"
prop::collection::vec generates vectors of random elements
Custom strategies with prop_compose! for complex types
Think about properties: commutativity, associativity, round-tripping, idempotency
